description: Here's the Python implementation of the Bloom filter factory: ```python from org.apache.lucene.codecs.bloom import BloomFilterFactory, FuzzySet class DefaultBloomFilterFactory(BloomFilterFactory): def get_set_for_field(self, state, info): # Assume all of the docs have low doc-frequency return FuzzySet.createSetBasedOnQuality(state.segmentInfo.maxDoc, 0.10) def downsize(self, info, initial_set): # Aim for ~10% saturation target_size = int(initial_set.size() * 0.10) return initial_set.downsize(target_size) def is_saturated(self, set, info): # Save the filter if more than 90% of its bits are set return set.size() < (set.size() * 0.90) ``` The `get_set_for_field` method creates a Bloom filter with a specified saturation level. The saturation level is set to 0.10, which means that the Bloom filter will be expected to have 10% of its bits set. The `downsize` method aims for a bitset size that would have 10% of bits set. It takes a `FieldInfo` object and an initial `FuzzySet` object as input and returns a smaller bitset that is hopefully more densely packed. The `is_saturated` method decides whether to save the Bloom filter. It takes a `FuzzySet` object and a `FieldInfo` object as input and returns a boolean value indicating whether the Bloom filter is saturated. A Bloom filter is considered saturated if more than 90% of its bits are set. The code depends on the `org.apache.lucene.index.FieldInfo` and `org.apache.lucene.index.SegmentWriteState` classes from the Apache Lucene library. It also uses the `FuzzySet` class, which is a custom implementation of a Bloom filter.
